div1.entryFree:
Endeared, 1) made dear, raised in price: thy bosom is e. with all hearts, which I by lacking have supposed dead, Sonn. 31, 1. to be e. to a king, John IV, 2, 228.
2) bound, obliged: you broke your word, when you were more e. to it than now, H4B II, 3, 11. we are so virtuously bound, so infinitely e. Tim. I, 2, 233. I am so much e. to that lord; he's ever sending, III, 2, 36.
